retrieved_docs.title: Glioblastoma Multiforme -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials
retrieved_docs.content: Studies have shown patients who had RT doses of 50 Gy to 60 Gy had longer median survival than those who received lower postoperative RT doses. [48] [49] Studies have also shown marginal or no benefit of brachytherapy for high-grade gliomas, given their infiltrative nature. [51] [52] Studies testing the efficacy of proton and neutron therapy are underway. Radiation sensitizers are the compounds given along with radiotherapy to increase its therapeutic effect. None of these compounds are approved for GBM at present.

retrieved_docs.title: Astrocytoma -- Medical Oncology -- Antineoplastic Agents
retrieved_docs.content: Temozolomide is an oral alkylating agent converted to 3-methyl-(triazen-1-yl)imidazole-4-carboxamide (MTIC) at physiologic pH. This agent is 100% bioavailable, and approximately 35% crosses the blood-brain barrier. Based on the CATNON trial, adjuvant treatment consists of 12 monthly cycles, beginning 1 month after radiotherapy completion. The first cycle dose is 150 mg/m 2 , followed by 200 mg/m 2 orally for 5 days in a 28-day cycle. Temozolomide is moderately emetogenic; hence, antiemetic prophylaxis is required with 5-HT3 antagonists. The Procarbazine, Lomustine, and Vincristine (PCV) regimen involves a 6- to 8-week cycle length for 6 cycles. Eight-week cycles may be preferred for grade 2 tumors. [21] mTOR inhibitors are also proposed for the treatment of grade 1 astrocytomas.

retrieved_docs.title: Bevacizumab -- Indications -- FDA-approved Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Glioblastoma: Bevacizumab has approval as a single agent in patients with progressive glioblastoma following previous therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: Low-Grade Gliomas -- Medical Oncology
retrieved_docs.content: In the setting of recurrent disease, systemic treatments for recurrent LGG can be considered alone, concurrent with radiotherapy, or adjuvantly. PCV, TMZ, or lomustine alone can be considered. PCV or TMZ can be considered after radiotherapy as well. Platinum-based therapies may also be used in cases of progression through other first-line treatments. The TAVAVEC trial investigating the combination of bevacizumab with TMZ in recurrent WHO Grade II and III gliomas failed to demonstrate any improvement in overall or progression-free survival. Another phase 3 trial is conducted to compare RT with adjuvant temozolomide versus RT with adjuvant PCV in anaplastic or low-grade co-deleted gliomas. (NCT00887146) Clinical trials targeting the mutant IDH protein are also underway. [54]

retrieved_docs.title: Phenothiazine -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Also, recent studies have investigated the anti-cancer properties of phenothiazines in glioblastoma as it produced an arrest in the G1 cycle of cancer cells, pointing to a reduction in cyclins stimulating DNA replication along with an increase in Cycline inhibiting compounds. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Low-Grade Gliomas -- Medical Oncology
retrieved_docs.content: Targeted therapies such as BRAF/MEK inhibitors may be appropriate in certain gliomas, such as pilocytic astrocytomas, pleomorphic xanthoastrocytoma (PXA), and ganglioneuromas. Patients that are incompletely resected or have progression with a BRAF V600E activating mutations may be eligible for Dabrafenib/trametinib or vemurafenib/cobimetinib. These drugs arrest cell growth by inhibiting B-Raf (dabrafenib and vemurafenib) and MEK (trametinib and cobimetinib), which are part of the Ras-Raf-MEK-ERK pathway that stimulates cell proliferation. For subependymal giant cell astrocytomas, an mTOR inhibitor such as everolimus may be considered.

retrieved_docs.title: Astrocytoma -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Recommendations for recurrent tumors include reoperation, carmustine wafers, alternate chemotherapeutic regimens, and investigational IDH -targeted approaches. Re-radiation is rarely helpful; an additional 36 Gy in 18 fractions may be given. Bevacizumab, a humanized VEGF monoclonal antibody, has activity in recurrent glioblastoma, potentially increasing progression-free survival (PFS) and reducing peritumoral edema and glucocorticoid use. [12] IDH -targeted approaches, including vorasidenib and other small-molecule inhibitors of mutant IDH1 and 2 (ivosidenib, olutasidenib, safusedinib), are in trials. Vaccine strategies are also being explored. Recurrent glioblastoma treatment decisions must take into consideration factors such as previous therapy, time to relapse, performance status, and quality of life. Whenever possible, patients with recurrent disease should be enrolled in clinical trials.

retrieved_docs.title: Glioblastoma Multiforme -- Medical Oncology
retrieved_docs.content: According to NCCN guidelines, patients with newly diagnosed MGMT-methylated glioblastoma, aged 70 years or younger, should be treated with TMZ and radiotherapy. Trials have shown increased overall survival at 2 and 5 years on continued follow-up in a group who received radiotherapy with concurrent daily TMZ followed by 6 monthly cycles of adjuvant TMZ. Results are similar in patients aged older than 60 and with poor prognostic factors. [55] [56] [57] Trials were conducted with a combined regimen of TMZ and lomustine with radiation therapy as an alternative option in MGMT-methylated GBM, which resulted in inconclusive results. [58]

retrieved_docs.title: Gliomas -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Clinical trials : Consider enrollment in clinical trials to access novel therapies and contribute to advancing treatment options.

retrieved_docs.title: Glioblastoma Multiforme -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Due to the aggressive nature of the tumor, palliative care should be initiated as soon as the diagnosis is made. It is essential to determine the goals of care and patients' wishes throughout the continuum of care. Patients and family members must discuss prognosis to set expectations about the disease. The overall goal is to maintain the patient's quality of life as long as possible with adequate management of their symptomatology. Nevertheless, discussions regarding newer therapeutic avenues and clinical trials should be had in eligible patients. [67]

retrieved_docs.title: Glioblastoma Multiforme -- Medical Oncology
retrieved_docs.content: Treatment is similar in MGMT-unmethylated glioblastoma patients aged 70 years or younger; however, these patients derive less benefit from TMZ when compared to the methylated group. Standard therapy with TMZ and radiotherapy is recommended if MGMT status is unknown due to the benefit from TMZ with a tolerable adverse effect profile, along with the lack of available alternatives for unmethylated tumors.

retrieved_docs.title: Low-Grade Gliomas -- Medical Oncology
retrieved_docs.content: On further follow-up, the 5 and 10-year overall survival was 60.9% and 34.6%. [48] The drug is delivered at 75mg/m given with radiotherapy and 150mg/m post-radiation. The most common toxicity is myelosuppression, as well as nausea and vomiting.

retrieved_docs.title: Afatinib -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Saran F. et al conducted a clinical trial in patients with glioblastoma multiforme to determine the maximum tolerated dose of afatinib in combination with radiotherapy and with or without temozolomide. The maximum tolerated dose of afatinib was 30 mg daily with radiotherapy and temozolomide and 40 mg of afatinib with radiotherapy. [7] Afatinib is being examined for treating HER2-positive gastric cancer. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Astrocytoma -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Consider enrolling eligible patients in clinical trials to access cutting-edge therapies and contribute to advancing treatment options.

retrieved_docs.title: Glioblastoma Multiforme -- Medical Oncology
retrieved_docs.content: In patients aged 70 years or older with good performance status, TMZ and radiotherapy are recommended, but a hypofractionated radiation course can be done rather than the standard course. Twelve cycles of adjuvant TMZ are recommended with hypofractionated radiotherapy rather than 6 cycles in such patients. [59] In patients aged 70 years or older with poor performance status, a single modality, either TMZ or radiotherapy, can be considered to avoid adverse effects and toxicities. MGMT methylation status can help decide between chemotherapy and radiotherapy in such cases. [60] No substantial evidence exists for alternatives to TMZ for MGMT-unmethylated tumors, although trials have been conducted using a combination of bevacizumab/irinotecan in the past. [61]

retrieved_docs.title: Glioblastoma Multiforme -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Developing comprehensive treatment plans tailored to each patient's unique circumstances is essential. This involves considering factors such as tumor location, extent of resection, molecular characteristics, patient preferences, and comorbidities. Strategic decision-making ensures optimal therapeutic outcomes while minimizing adverse effects and maximizing quality of life. Ethical considerations are paramount in GBM care, particularly regarding treatment decisions, end-of-life care, and participation in clinical trials.

Collectively, these relationships provide a cohesive view of how individual anticancer drugs and other pharmacologic agents are organized within treatment protocols, therapeutic classes, and disease‑specific applications, supporting informed patient care and precision oncology.

retrieved_docs.content: the experimental compound PD‑134308 functions simultaneously as a hormonal antagonist and an antitumor agent directed against the target isa, exemplifying dual pharmacologic activity. This integrated framework delineates classifications, qualifying relationships, therapeutic scopes and specific contraindications of key antineoplastic and inhibitory drugs, supporting informed oncologic patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 2 -- Community 531
retrieved_docs.content: lymphoma cells; pembrolizumab, a PD‑1 checkpoint inhibitor, enhances antitumor immune responses; and sargramostim, a recombinant granulocyte‑macrophage colony‑stimulating factor, can augment host immunity. Collectively, these agents—ranging from traditional chemotherapeutics and central‑nervous‑system depressants to modern immunotherapies—illustrate the comprehensive pharmacologic approach to Hodgkin disease, emphasizing individualized regimen selection to optimize patient outcomes and underscoring the importance of systematic drug classification for clinical research and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 1484
retrieved_docs.content: Pharmacologic Substance is a broad therapeutic class that encompasses a diverse array of agents, each of which is recognized as a specific value for a GDC (Genomic Data Commons) property. Among these agents are ethinylestradiol, a synthetic estrogen used in hormonal therapies; chlorambucil, an alkylating agent employed in the treatment of certain leukemias and lymphomas; chlormethin hydrochlorid, a chlorinated phenylurea herbicide with pharmacologic relevance; cyclophosphamide anhydrous, a widely used alkylating chemotherapy drug; dakarcazin, a nitrosourea compound with antineoplastic activity; hydrochlorid prokarbazinu (hydrochloride procarbazine), an oral chemotherapeutic agent; ifosfamid (ifosfamide), another alkylating agent used for solid tumors; lomustin (lomustine), a nitrosourea used in brain tumor therapy; pembrolizumab, a monoclonal antibody that blocks PD‑1 to enhance immune response against cancer; and vinblastine sulfate, a vinca alkaloid that interferes with microtubule formation in cancer cells. In addition, barbituráty (barbiturates) are identified as a subclass (isa) of Pharmacologic Substance, indicating that they belong to this overarching category. Collectively, these substances illustrate the extensive range of pharmacologic agents catalogued under a common GDC property, highlighting their varied mechanisms of action and therapeutic applications in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1290
retrieved_docs.content: Cabozantinib and gilteritinib are both classified as members of the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) group designated “Other protein kinase inhibitors,” indicating that each drug belongs to this specific category of protein kinase–targeting agents. This classification reflects their shared mechanism of action as protein kinase inhibitors within the broader ATC system.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 1 -- Community 64
retrieved_docs.content: used in oncology (dexamethasone phosphate and the prednisolone esters listed above). Understanding these interconnections—hierarchical classifications within ATC, dual membership of agents such as anagrelide, specific mechanisms of action, therapeutic indications for leukemia, and contraindications for certain oncolytic agents—is essential for clinicians when selecting chemotherapy regimens, anticipating drug‑specific effects, and managing patient care in oncology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 329
retrieved_docs.content: The ATC category “Other protein kinase inhibitors in ATC” comprises the kinase‑targeted agents cabozantinib, gilteritinib and midostaurin. Both gilteritinib and midostaurin are indicated as potential therapies for acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد). As members of the same protein‑kinase inhibitor class, these drugs share a common mechanism of action that involves inhibition of specific signaling pathways driving malignant cell proliferation, and their use in AML highlights the therapeutic relevance of kinase inhibition in hematologic malignancies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1919
retrieved_docs.content: Pharmacologic substances represent a broad class of therapeutic agents that includes a diverse array of specific compounds. Ethinylestradiol is a pharmacologic substance used primarily as an estrogen component in hormonal contraceptives. Barbituráty are classified as a type of pharmacologic substance, reflecting their role as central nervous system depressants. Bleomycin sulfate, brentuximab vedotin, chlorambucil, dakarbazin, and doxorubicin hydrochloride are pharmacologic substances employed as chemotherapeutic agents in the treatment of various cancers. Pembrolizumab is a pharmacologic substance that functions as an immune‑checkpoint inhibitor, enhancing antitumor immune responses. Prokarbazin, sargramostim, and vinorelbin tartarát (vinorelbine tartrate) are also pharmacologic substances, with sargramostim acting as a cytokine growth factor and vinorelbin tartarát used as a chemotherapy drug. Together, these examples illustrate the extensive range of pharmacologic substances that clinicians utilize across multiple therapeutic areas, highlighting their importance in patient care and disease management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 327
retrieved_docs.content: Canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab are both classified as epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors. As members of the EGFR inhibitor class, these agents act on the EGFR signaling pathway, which plays a critical role in the growth and survival of certain cancer cells. Recognizing that both canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab share this mechanism of action helps clinicians select appropriate targeted therapies and anticipate similar pharmacologic effects and potential adverse events associated with EGFR inhibition.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1437
retrieved_docs.content: In radiation biology, each qualifier can modulate, enhance, mitigate, or otherwise influence physiological responses to ionizing and non‑ionizing radiation. Hypoxanthine’s PARP‑inhibitory activity specifically suppresses DNA‑repair mechanisms, affecting cellular recovery after radiation‑induced damage. Recognizing these agents as radiation modifiers informs the design of protective strategies, therapeutic regimens, and risk assessments for both therapeutic radiation exposure and accidental radiation incidents. Collectively, the inclusion of these permissible qualifiers facilitates standardized manufacturing, regulatory compliance, targeted therapeutic development, and a comprehensive understanding of immunological, metabolic, and radiobiological processes, thereby improving patient care and advancing biomedical research.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1573
retrieved_docs.content: Pharmacologic Substance is a broad category that includes a diverse array of agents such as ethinylestradiol, barbiturates, bleomycin sulfate, brentuximab vedotin, chlormethin hydrochloride, procarbazine hydrochloride, ifosfamide, lomustine, pembrolizumab, sargramostim, and vinblastine sulfate. These compounds represent multiple therapeutic classes: ethinylestradiol is a synthetic estrogen used in hormonal therapy; barbiturates are central‑nervous‑system depressants; bleomycin sulfate, ifosfamide, vinblastine sulfate, and lomustine are cytotoxic chemotherapeutic agents; brentuximab vedotin and pembrolizumab are monoclonal antibodies that target specific cancer antigens and immune checkpoints; sargramostim is a recombinant cytokine that stimulates granulocyte‑macrophage colony formation; and chlormethin hydrochloride and procarbazine hydrochloride are alkylating agents used in oncology. All of these substances are recorded as values for a specific data‑catalog property associated with the Pharmacologic Substance class, highlighting their shared classification while underscoring their distinct mechanisms of action and clinical applications.
